summary: Iteration 238 regression — Events seed verification + Bulk Actions roll-out to 8 management pages. Backend: 10/10 tests passing (3 events surface via /api/events with proper shape; the new whitelist entries banquets / pressings / package_services accept admin bulk calls and reject customer 403). Frontend: 2/7 card-grid+table pages fully verified end-to-end (Restaurants & Banquet — bulk-actions-bar appears with Activate/Deactivate/Export on first checkbox click). 1 page broken (Hotels). 4 pages need investigation (Cars / Laundry / Packages / Travel — bulk-select-all testid not found on the Management sub-tab — most likely empty table in this dev DB, so the BulkSelectHeader column doesn't render). Cinema legacy bulk-delete UI confirmed untouched (no new bulk-actions-bar surfaced — correct).

ui_bugs.issue: Cards render in LIST view by default (horizontal layout via `List` toggle). 9 bulk-select-row checkboxes ARE mounted (data-testid present, count visible), BUT Playwright `.check()` reports "Clicking the checkbox did not change its state" — the absolute-positioned overlay at `top-2 left-2 z-20` is being covered/eaten by the hotel image or another stacking element on the list-view card. Toggling to Grid view may fix it, but the checkbox MUST be reachable in the default view, otherwise users with list-view as default cannot bulk-select.

integration_issues.issue: After clicking the entity sub-tab (Fleet Management / Management / Services / Management), the page does NOT expose `cars-bulk-select-all`, `pressings-bulk-select-all`, `packages-bulk-select-all`, `routes-bulk-select-all`, or `travel-vehicles-bulk-select-all` testids. Two likely root causes: (a) the BulkSelectHeader column is conditionally rendered only when `rows.length > 0`, and the dev DB is empty for those tabs at this moment; or (b) the testid is on a parent th/td that isn't getting attached. Main agent should verify by either: (i) confirming the table HAS rows for superadmin@oryno.com — if empty, seed a couple of cars/pressings/packages/routes; (ii) inspecting the wired pages to confirm BulkSelectHeader is unconditionally rendered.

The page opens with a 'Discover Events' SEARCH FORM (City, Event Type, Date, Tickets, Search button) instead of a default list of all events. Backend has 3 seeded events that satisfy start_date >= today + correct event_type, but customer must perform a Search Events click to surface cards. Confirm intended UX — if events should be listed by default for first-time visitors (so EventBooking is discoverable without a city filter), this needs a default `GET /api/events/` fetch on mount.

action_items: Fix Hotels list-view checkbox interactivity. `BulkSelectCardWrapper`'s `<input type='checkbox'>` is mounted but unreachable in HotelCard's List layout. Either (a) bump the wrapper's z-index higher than the hotel-image element, (b) ensure pointer-events on the parent card don't swallow the click, or (c) move the checkbox to the leftmost column in list view.

Either render `BulkSelectHeader` even when the table is empty (so the header testid is always discoverable), OR seed a few rows for cars/pressings/packages/travel-routes in the dev DB so the bulk header surfaces. Currently Cars/Laundry/Packages/Travel cannot be verified.

Decide default UX for /services/events — if cards should auto-list (not gated behind a search), add a default fetch on mount in EventsPage. The 3 seeded events ARE returned by /api/events/?limit=10 (verified).
Optional: investigate the recurring console warning 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0...' coming from recharts in the dashboard tabs — cosmetic but noisy.
critical_code_review_comments: BulkSelectCardWrapper component is small (20 lines) and clean — onChange/onClick both stopPropagation, so it should NOT propagate to the card. Works on Restaurants & Banquet. The Hotels failure is therefore a STACKING-CONTEXT / pointer-events problem in the consuming HotelCard, not in the shared wrapper.

seed_events.py is idempotent (skips by name+operator_id) and correctly uses string ISO dates (`start_date >= today` filter passes). The 3 events have valid ticket_types arrays with numeric prices and the expected event_type set (festival/conference/party).

admin_bulk.py whitelist additions (banquets / pressings / package_services) verified end-to-end via pytest: superadmin call returns 200 with modified=0 for a non-existent id; customer call returns 403.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Confirmed seed_events.py output is live in dev DB (3 events). No bulk delete/deactivate was executed against real data (per safety rule) — testing was limited to selecting cards and confirming the bulk-actions-bar surfaces.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is 100% green. Use /app/backend/tests/test_iter238_events_seed_and_bulk.py for regression. Frontend: Restaurants (8 cards) and Banquet (12 cards) bulk flows are fully working — clicking any [data-testid^='bulk-select-row-'] surfaces [data-testid='bulk-actions-bar'] with Activate/Deactivate/Export buttons + bulk-count=1. Hotels has 9 bulk-card overlays mounted but the checkbox is non-interactive in default LIST view — try toggling the 'Grid' view button at the top-right of the hotel list area to confirm if Grid view works (it likely does). For Cars/Laundry/Packages/Travel — first verify tables have data; if empty, seed a couple rows then re-run. Cinema is intentionally untouched — its custom bulk-delete UI (Set-based selectedCinemaIds + 'Delete (N)' button at the top of the Cinemas tab) is preserved; no [data-testid='bulk-actions-bar'] should appear there.
rca_of_the_issue: Hotels checkbox failure RCA: BulkSelectCardWrapper renders an `<input type='checkbox'>` inside `<div className='absolute top-2 left-2 z-20'>`. On Restaurant/Banquet cards this wins the z-stack and is clickable. On HotelCard (List variant) the underlying image element or card-padding overlay either (a) sits at a higher z-index due to its parent forming a new stacking context, or (b) has pointer-events absorbing the click via a `<Link>` wrapper. Confirmed by Playwright reporting 'Clicking the checkbox did not change its state' — the input received a click but its `change` event did not fire (event was likely swallowed before reaching the input).
